input.prompt: I want to build a real-time collaborative dashboard where users can see live updates. What .NET project templates could work? I need you to search available templates and show me the top 3 options with their key parameters.

data.content: I want to build a real-time collaborative dashboard where users can see live updates. What .NET project templates could work? I need you to search available templates and show me the top 3 options with their key parameters.
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-05T00:54:33.917Z</current_datetime>

I want to build a real-time collaborative dashboard where users can see live updates. What .NET project templates could work? I need you to search available templates and show me the top 3 options with their key parameters.

input.initialPrompt: I want to build a real-time collaborative dashboard where users can see live updates. What .NET project templates could work? I need you to search available templates and show me the top 3 options with their key parameters.

arguments.command: dotnet new search "blazor" --columns "Template Name,Short Name,Type,Language" 2>/dev/null | head -30
dotnet new search "signalr" --columns "Template Name,Short Name,Type,Language" 2>/dev/null | head -20
dotnet new search "webapp" --columns "Template Name,Short Name,Type,Language" 2>/dev/null | head -20

data.reasoningText: The user wants to find .NET project templates that could work for a real-time collaborative dashboard. I'll search for available .NET templates using `dotnet new list` or `dotnet new search` commands.

arguments.command: dotnet new search "blazor" --columns "Template Name,Short Name,Type,Language" 2>/dev/null | head -30
dotnet new search "signalr" --columns "Template Name,Short Name,Type,Language" 2>/dev/null | head -20
dotnet new search "webapp" --columns "Template Name,Short Name,Type,Language" 2>/dev/null | head -20

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet new search \"blazor\" --columns \"Template Name,Short Name,Type,Language\" 2>/dev/null | head -30", "description": "Search for Blazor project templates", "initial_wait": 15}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet new search \"signalr\" --columns \"Template Name,Short Name,Type,Language\" 2>/dev/null | head -20", "description": "Search for SignalR project templates", "initial_wait": 15}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet new search \"webapp\" --columns \"Template Name,Short Name,Type,Language\" 2>/dev/null | head -20", "description": "Search for webapp project templates", "initial_wait": 15}

toolArgs.command: dotnet new search "signalr" --columns "Template Name,Short Name,Type,Language" 2>/dev/null | head -20

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
Description:
  Searches for the templates on NuGet.org.

Usage:
  dotnet new search [<template-name>] [options]

Arguments:
  <template-name>  If specified, only the templates matching the name will be shown.

Options:
  --author <author>                      Filters the templates based on the template author.
  -lang, --language <language>           Filters templates based on language.
  --type <type>                          Filters templates based on available types. Predefined values are "project" 
                                         and "item".
  --tag <tag>                            Filters the templates based on the tag.
  --package <package>                    Filters the templates based on NuGet package ID.
  --columns-all                          Displays all columns in the output. [default: False]
  --columns <author|language|tags|type>  Specifies the columns to display in the output.
  -v, --verbosity <LEVEL>                Sets the verbosity level. Allowed values are q[uiet], m[inimal], n[ormal], and 
                                         diag[nostic]. [default: normal]
<exited with exit code 0>

arguments.description: Check dotnet new list header format

data.reasoningText: The `dotnet new search` command seems to not be returning results properly. Let me try listing locally installed templates instead, or try the search with different syntax.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
--dry-run               Displays a summary of what would happen if the given command line were run if it would result 
  --force                 Forces content to be generated even if it would change existing files. [default: False]
  --no-update-check       Disables checking for the template package updates when instantiating a template. [default: 
  --project <project>     The project that should be used for context evaluation.
  --type <project>        Specifies the template type to instantiate.
  --no-restore                             If specified, skips the automatic restore of the project on create.
  --authority <authority>                  The authority of the OIDC provider (use with standalone Individual auth).
  --aad-b2c-instance <aad-b2c-instance>    The Azure Active Directory B2C instance to connect to (use with 
  --aad-instance <aad-instance>            The Azure Active Directory instance to connect to (use with SingleOrg auth).
  --client-id <client-id>                  The Client ID for this project (use with IndividualB2C, SingleOrg or 
  --domain <domain>                        The domain for the directory tenant (use with SingleOrg or IndividualB2C 
  --app-id-uri <app-id-uri>                The App ID Uri for the server API we want to call (use with SingleOrg or 
  --api-client-id <api-client-id>          The Client ID for the API that the server hosts (use with IndividualB2C, 
  --tenant-id <tenant-id>                  The TenantId ID of the directory to connect to (use with SingleOrg auth).
  --exclude-launch-settings                Whether to exclude launchSettings.json from the generated template.
<exited with exit code 0>

toolArgs.command: dotnet new blazor --help 2>/dev/null | grep -A2 "Interactive render mode\|--interactivity\|--use-server\|--auth" | head -20

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
-int, --interactivity <None|Server|...>  Chooses which interactive render mode to use for interactive components
                                           Type: choice
                                             None         No interactivity (static server rendering only)
--
  -au, --auth <Individual|None>            The type of authentication to use
                                           Type: choice
                                             None        No authentication
--
  -uld, --use-local-db                     Whether to use LocalDB instead of SQLite. This option only applies if --auth 
                                           Individual is specified.
                                           Type: bool
--
                                           for --auth.
